title: spiranthes macrophylla
intro: Spiranthes macrophylla, also known as the large-flowered ladies' tresses, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to North America. It is a perennial plant with long, thin leaves and white, waxy flowers that bloom in late summer. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Spiranthes macrophylla can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of menopause.
